steps.title: Find the first unknown angle
steps.content:
In triangle $ABC$, the angles at $A$ and $B$ are $20^\circ$ and $100^\circ$. Let $z$ be the angle at $C$:
$$20^\circ+100^\circ+z=180^\circ$$
$$z=60^\circ$$

steps.title: Use vertical angles
steps.content: Segments $AD$ and $BE$ intersect at $C$, so the opposite angle in triangle $CDE$ also measures $60^\circ$.

steps.title: Finish the second triangle
steps.content:
In triangle $CDE$, the known angles are $60^\circ$ and $40^\circ$:
$$60^\circ+40^\circ+y=180^\circ$$
$$y=80$$
The answer is C.
